title: Syncarpha milleflora
intro: Syncarpha milleflora, also known as the Cape marigold, is a flowering plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Syncarpha milleflora include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Syncarpha milleflora is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Syncarpha milleflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the tribe Astereae, which includes various species of daisies, asters, and other related plants. Syncarpha milleflora is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa, where it grows in a variety of habitats. The plant is known for its clusters of small, white or pink flowers, which are highly fragrant and attractive to pollinators. Syncarpha milleflora is a popular choice among gardeners and conservationists due to its beauty and hardiness in the face of drought and other environmental stressors.
